§ Enjoying

Enjoying

Books, records, films, podcasts. The things I keep coming back to, lightly annotated.

  • § Music · 2023

    Water Made Us

    Jamila Woods

    Wrote most of the first year at Superhuman to this. Soft enough to not intrude, dense enough to reward attention when you give it.
  • § Music · 2022

    Farm to Table

    Bartees Strange

    A lesson in refusing genre. I play \"Heavy Heart\" the night before hard meetings.
  • § Music · 2020

    Fetch the Bolt Cutters

    Fiona Apple

    The album that taught me percussion is a thought, not an accompaniment. Still unbeaten as a study in what a voice can carry.
  • § Music · 2019

    U.F.O.F.

    Big Thief

    The record I reach for when I need the world to slow down by about 20%. Adrianne and Buck, again.
  • § Music · 2017

    Ctrl

    SZA

    Went in expecting a hit album, left with a slow burn. \"20 Something\" is still the closer.
  • § Music · 2014

    Are We There

    Sharon Van Etten

    Soundtracked a move from London to the Bay. \"Your Love is Killing Me\" did most of the lifting.
  • § Music · 2007

    In Rainbows

    Radiohead

    The first album I paid for with my own money, in the pay-what-you-want days. Still finds new ways to be the right record.
  • § Music · 2001

    The Blueprint

    Jay-Z

    I was 14, and my older cousin handed me a burned CD. Changed what I thought a record could feel like on headphones.
  • § Reading · Now reading

    The Craftsman

    Richard Sennett

    Second read. The chapter on the hand is doing most of the work.
  • § Reading · Now reading

    Design Justice

    Sasha Costanza-Chock

    Slow read — one chapter a week, with notes.
  • § Reading · Now reading

    A Web for Everyone

    Horton & Quesenbery

    Rereading for a workshop.
  • § Reading · Queued

    Mismatch

    Kat Holmes

    Borrowed from a colleague.
